Definition: Breaking down complex information to find what's really going on — you're not satisfied until you understand the structure beneath the surface.
Pulling information from many sources into a coherent whole — you see the pattern when others see only pieces.
Identifying the root cause of a problem, not just its symptoms — you find what others miss and name it with precision.
Constructing something lasting — a product, a team, a system — you're energized by the work of making something real from nothing.
Making sure the right things happen in the right order across multiple people or workstreams — you're the person who holds it all together.
Reducing tension, conflict, or crisis before it escalates — you read the room accurately and know what to say to bring the temperature down.
Taking responsibility for how a team, system, or process runs — you're energized by ownership and good at keeping things on track.
Taking a problem and working it through to a resolution — you're energized by the puzzle and satisfied only when it's genuinely cracked.
Helping others understand something they didn't before — you find deep satisfaction when the concept clicks and someone gets it.
Getting ideas across clearly so others understand — you match your message to your audience and leave no room for confusion.
Generating vivid mental pictures of things that don't exist yet — your mind naturally goes to what could be, not just what is.
Shaping how others think, feel, or act without using authority — you move people through ideas, credibility, and genuine connection.
Making a compelling case that moves someone toward your point of view — you know how to sequence an argument and you read your audience.
Thinking through what needs to happen and building a clear path to get there — you feel most confident when you have a solid plan.
Bringing something new into existence — you feel most alive when you're making something that wasn't there before.
Generating a high volume of ideas freely and without judgment — you're energized by open possibility and the creative collision of concepts.
Thinking several moves ahead and designing the path from here to the goal — you see the whole board while others focus on the next move.
Setting the course and making the calls — you're decisive and energized by the responsibility of pointing the way forward.
Finding better ways to do things and making them real — you're energized by the gap between how things are and how they could be.
Conceiving something genuinely new — you don't just improve what exists, you imagine what doesn't.
Shaping form and function into something that works and feels right — you care equally about how something operates and how it's experienced.
Generating a stream of ideas quickly and freely — you're energized by quantity and trust that volume produces the gem.
Trying new approaches with curiosity and discipline — you treat every attempt as data and learn as much from what fails as what works.
Building a rough version of something to learn from it — you'd rather have something imperfect to react to than a perfect plan on paper.
Doing work with high attention to skill and finish — you care about the quality of the thing itself, not just that it's done.
Taking something that exists and envisioning it as fundamentally different — you question the premise, not just the execution.
Shifting the lens on a problem so a new solution becomes visible — you offer perspective that changes what's possible.
Creating in the moment without a plan — you're energized by spontaneity and do some of your best work without a script.
Venturing into unfamiliar territory — ideas, places, domains — with curiosity and open attention.
Uncovering something that was previously unknown or unrecognized — you live for the moment a hidden truth comes into view.
Going first into unexplored territory — you're energized by the unfamiliar and willing to figure it out without a map.
Turning information, experience, or ideas into a narrative that lands — you know that a great story changes minds when data alone doesn't.
Creating mental or actual images that communicate what words alone can't — you think in pictures and help others see.
Producing a high volume of output — ideas, options, content, solutions — you're a natural source when others run dry.
Adjusting quickly and effectively when circumstances change — you don't need things to stay the same to perform well.
Driving fundamental change in how something looks, operates, or is understood — you operate at the level of root-and-branch, not incremental adjustment.
Coordinating many moving parts into a unified, well-timed result — you conduct the whole thing so it sounds like one coherent performance.
Painting a compelling picture of a desirable future — you help others see where they could go, not just where they are.
Defining how a problem, idea, or opportunity is understood and presented — you know that how something is framed determines how it's received.
Finding your way through complex, ambiguous, or shifting terrain — you're calm in the maze and good at finding the way out.
Changing course decisively when the data or situation demands it — you can let go of a direction without losing momentum.
Finding the most efficient path to a result — you're never satisfied with 'good enough' when 'better' is within reach.
Stripping complexity down to its clearest form — you make hard things understandable without losing what matters.
Removing what's unnecessary to make a process faster, simpler, or more effective — you're energized by elimination as much as addition.
Turning one-off processes into repeatable systems — you see patterns in what you do and build the infrastructure to do it consistently.
Imposing clear organization on complex or ambiguous material — you build the scaffold that lets others climb.
Establishing and maintaining the rules, oversight, and accountability structures that keep things sound — you care about doing things the right way.
Deciding what matters most and ordering effort accordingly — you make hard calls about what to do and, just as importantly, what not to do.
Bringing people, goals, and resources into coherent direction — you spot misalignment before it becomes dysfunction.
Determining the right order for things to happen — you think in dependencies and understand that sequence is often strategy.
Defining where something or someone stands relative to everything else — you understand competitive landscape and know how to carve out ground.
Seeing what's coming before it arrives and preparing for it — you're rarely surprised because you've already thought it through.
Projecting what's likely to happen next by reading current signals — you think in scenarios and help others prepare for what's coming.
Creating a clear picture of how things relate and connect — you make the invisible structure visible so others can navigate it.
Measuring performance against the best available standard — you instinctively ask 'compared to what?' and use that answer to drive improvement.
Confirming that something is sound, accurate, or ready — you're the person who checks the work and catches what slipped through.
Running experiments and checks to see what actually holds up — you trust verified results over confident assumptions.
Defining what success looks like and quantifying whether you're achieving it — you believe that what gets measured gets managed.
Weighing evidence and options with clear judgment — you cut through noise and land on what actually matters.
Taking a clear-eyed look at a situation, capability, or risk — you evaluate honestly and don't let preference distort what you see.
Making sense of data, signals, or events to extract meaningful insight — you tell others what the information actually says.
Working through problems step by step with logical rigor — you trust argument over intuition and build conclusions that hold.
Diving deep into a subject to surface the truth — you're energized by the hunt and know how to find what isn't obvious.
Following a trail of evidence wherever it leads — you're drawn to unanswered questions and won't stop until you have answers.
Challenging assumptions and asking what everyone else takes for granted — the right question, to you, is more powerful than the obvious answer.
Building a detailed picture of a person, market, or situation — you read patterns and assemble them into something actionable.
Identifying what makes one thing distinctly valuable or different from another — you see nuance where others see sameness.
Drawing conclusions from incomplete information through careful logical thought — you're comfortable working with less than perfect data.
Reducing something complex to its most essential form — you find the core insight buried in the volume and surface it cleanly.
Taking people somewhere they couldn't get to without you — you're energized by the responsibility and the trust that comes with it.
Lighting a fire in others — you communicate possibility in a way that makes people want to be part of something larger than themselves.
Understanding what drives people and activating it — you find the engine in others and help them run at full speed.
Advocating fiercely for a person, idea, or cause — you lend your credibility and voice to what you believe in.
Giving others the authority, confidence, and resources to act — you build people up and step back so they can step forward.
Sharing hard-won experience to help someone navigate their path — you find deep meaning in being useful to someone earlier in their journey.
Drawing out the best in others through questions, challenge, and belief — you help people find answers within themselves.
Patiently developing something — a relationship, a talent, a culture — you take the long view and tend what others overlook.
Getting people moving in a common direction — you turn passive agreement into active effort.
Bringing together diverse people or perspectives into a cohesive whole — you find the common ground and make people feel they're on the same side.
Assigning the right work to the right people and trusting them to deliver — you know that great outcomes require letting go of control.
Raising the quality, ambition, or standard of what's around you — your presence lifts the work and the people doing it.
Speaking up for what you believe in, especially when it's not the easy position — you're willing to be the voice others won't be.
Pushing people and ideas to be better — you ask the hard question, hold the higher standard, and don't let good enough be enough.
Raising the vitality of the people and environment around you — your presence makes things feel more alive and possible.
Delivering ideas to a group with clarity and confidence — you prepare carefully and perform well when others are listening.
Breaking down complex ideas until others genuinely understand them — you feel the job isn't done until the concept clicks.
Expressing ideas fluently and precisely in spoken or written form — you give language to things others struggle to name.
Giving your full attention to what someone is saying — not just the words, but what's underneath — and making them feel genuinely heard.
Drawing out information, perspective, or stories through skilled questioning — you know how to put people at ease and get to what's real.
Creating the conditions for a group to think, decide, or create together — you hold the space without dominating it.
Capturing the key points of something complex in a brief, accurate form — you give others what they need without making them read everything.
Building capability in others through instruction and practice — you're energized when you see someone improve because of your investment.
Putting a decision, plan, or design into practice — you're energized by the moment strategy becomes action.
Getting things done and crossing the finish line — you take real satisfaction in completion and don't leave things half-finished.
Getting something new off the ground — you love the energy of the start, the first day, the moment something becomes real.
Translating plans into action with discipline and follow-through — you're the person who makes sure things actually happen.
Starting something from scratch — you're drawn to the blank page and energized by being first.
Bringing separate parts together into a functioning whole — you make systems work by connecting what was disconnected.
Designing and building systems that work — you think in mechanisms and find deep satisfaction in elegance under constraint.
Bringing a project, event, or piece of work from concept to completion — you're the person who makes it actually happen.
Taking something that works and making it work at much greater size — you think in systems and build for volume.
Identifying and resolving the root cause of a problem through systematic analysis — you're calm under malfunction and systematic in restoration.
Working through complicated or conflicted situations to restore clarity — you're patient with mess and good at finding the thread.
Restoring function, morale, or progress after a setback — you don't stay down long, and you know how to bring others back up with you.
Fixing what's broken — a relationship, a system, a reputation — you're willing to do the unglamorous work of restoration.
Bringing people in conflict to a resolution — you hold the space between them with neutrality and skill.
Finding mutually acceptable outcomes through skilled dialogue — you move parties toward agreement without losing what matters most.
Bringing a conflict, problem, or open question to a definitive conclusion — you don't leave loose ends.
Working with others toward a shared goal in a way that produces more than any of you could alone — you're a natural partner.
Building deep, sustained working relationships based on mutual investment — you're at your best when working closely with someone you trust.
Building and maintaining a wide web of professional relationships — you're genuinely interested in people and skilled at staying connected.
Feeling into another person's experience and letting it matter to you — you connect through genuine understanding, not just acknowledgment.
Being reliably present for others when they need it — you show up, you follow through, and people know they can count on you.
Orienting your effort toward the needs of others — you find meaning in being useful to people and causes that matter.
Attending to the wellbeing of others as a genuine priority — you notice when someone is struggling and you do something about it.
Creating an environment where others feel looked after and at home — you take real satisfaction in making others comfortable.
Bringing the right people together at the right time — you know that the meeting that matters most is the one you call.
Connecting people, ideas, or groups that wouldn't otherwise reach each other — you see paths across divides that others can't find.
Creating genuine relationships and bonds between people or organizations — you're energized by the link itself and what it makes possible.
Absorbing new knowledge, skills, and perspectives with genuine enthusiasm — you're never done and never bored when you're figuring something out.
Expanding capability, perspective, or impact over time through intentional effort — you're drawn to the gap between where you are and where you could be.
Moving something forward — a person, a cause, an initiative — you take what's there and push it further than it was.
Taking in large amounts of new information and processing it deeply — you're a sponge, and what you absorb becomes part of how you think.
Growing the reach, scope, or scale of something that's already working — you see potential where others see a ceiling.
Improving something iteratively until it's as good as it can be — you have a high standard and the patience to meet it.
Reaching goals through sustained effort — you set ambitious targets and take genuine satisfaction in hitting them.
Going beyond what was asked or expected — you're not satisfied with meeting the standard; you want to surpass it.
Speeding up progress by removing blockers, adding resources, or cutting what's slow — you move things faster and take energy from the pace.
Adding meaningful value to a team, project, or community — you're energized by being part of something and pulling your weight in it.
Committing resources now to create greater value later — you think in time horizons and are comfortable with delayed return.
Convincing others to put resources behind a vision — you translate possibility into commitment and make the numbers real.
Identifying and attracting the right people to join something — you know talent when you see it and know how to bring it in.
Making strong selection decisions that bring the right people into the right roles — you have a talent for seeing who will thrive.
Seeing and naming the contribution that others make, especially when it goes unnoticed — you make people feel seen.
Marking achievements, milestones, and contributions with genuine enthusiasm — you know that recognition matters and you give it freely.
Establishing consistent practices that can be repeated reliably — you build the playbook others follow.
Capturing knowledge, processes, and decisions in clear, accessible written form — you know that what isn't written down eventually disappears.
Providing well-considered guidance or suggestions based on expertise and analysis — you give others something they can actually act on.
Examining work systematically to assess quality, progress, or risk — you close the loop and make sure nothing slips through.
Improving something that already exists — cutting, sharpening, clarifying until it's as good as it can be.
Making the abstract concrete through examples, images, or demonstrations — you know that a great illustration is worth ten explanations.
Showing something in action to prove its value or teach its function — you'd rather show than tell.
Measuring yourself against others and being energized by the comparison — you perform better when there's something at stake.
Delivering something in front of an audience — a presentation, a pitch, a live moment — you come alive when others are watching.
Achieving the outcome in a competition or pursuit — you care about results and take real satisfaction in coming out on top.
Making a call when others are stuck — you're comfortable with ambiguity and willing to own the outcome of your choices.
Going all-in on a course of action — you give full effort and don't hedge when you've decided something is worth doing.
Completing what was started — you don't let things trail off, and there's real satisfaction in seeing something through to the end.
Turning intention into action — you're the spark that gets things moving and the force that keeps them going.
Moving forward despite uncertainty — you tolerate ambiguity and are willing to put something on the line in pursuit of what's possible.
Starting things — conversations, projects, relationships — you don't wait for permission or for someone else to go first.
Staying the course when things get hard — you have a longer tolerance for difficulty than most and you keep going when others stop.
Redesigning how something is organized to make it more effective — you're not afraid to change the bones of something that isn't working.
Allocating resources with discipline and foresight — you make sure the numbers work and that what matters gets funded.
Building representations of systems, markets, or scenarios to support decision-making — you think in models and trust what the structure reveals.
Creating belief in something and moving someone toward a decision — you connect what you offer to what the other person genuinely needs.
Shaping how an idea, product, or person is perceived by the people who matter — you understand audiences and know how to reach them.
Presenting an idea compellingly to someone who can say yes — you're energized by the moment of advocacy and good at making the case.
Engaging in structured argument to test ideas and find what's true — you sharpen your thinking through opposition and enjoy the contest.
Offering perspective and guidance to help others make better decisions — you're trusted for your judgment, not just your knowledge.
Applying your expertise to someone else's challenge and giving them a clear way forward — you're energized by the problem-and-answer exchange.
Recognizing and validating what's true and valuable in others — you see people clearly and tell them what you see.
Giving others the confidence to move forward — you believe in people, especially when they're struggling to believe in themselves.
Making people feel at ease and genuinely included from the first moment — you set the tone that makes others want to stay.
Putting what you know, have, or feel into the world generously — you believe that knowledge becomes more valuable when others have it too.
Stepping forward when something needs doing — you don't wait to be asked, and you give what the moment requires.
Picking up on what's happening beneath the surface — the mood, the subtext, the unspoken — before it becomes explicit.
Taking vague or diffuse thinking and making it sharp and clear — you turn fog into a precise, usable idea.
Changing the terms of a conversation, category, or problem — you don't accept the framing you're given if a better one is available.
Igniting something — an idea, a conversation, a movement — you're the initial catalyst that sets things in motion.
Putting forward a concrete idea, plan, or solution for consideration — you move conversations from discussion to decision.
Developing a skill or capability to a sharp edge — you're drawn to mastery and will work at something until it's excellent.
Running a small-scale test of something new — you're comfortable with uncertainty and know how to learn quickly from early runs.
Keeping something valuable going over the long term — you understand that maintenance is a form of leadership others undervalue.
Bringing steadiness to a situation that's volatile or uncertain — you're the calm that others organize around when things are shaky.
Facilitating deals, agreements, or exchanges between parties who need each other — you find the terms that make it work for everyone.
Watching carefully and noticing what others miss — you gather data through attention before you form a conclusion.
Creating physical or tangible things — you love the process of producing something real that you can hold, use, or point to.
Expressing ideas in language with precision and power — you think through writing and produce prose that moves people.
Creating joy, delight, or engagement for others — you know how to hold a room and you love the energy of doing it.
Offering genuine comfort and confidence to people who are worried or uncertain — you help others feel that things are going to be okay.
Searching out new talent, opportunity, or territory before others find it — you're energized by discovery and good at knowing what to look for.
Making something more robust, resilient, or capable — you build in the quality that makes things last.
Taking version one to version two — you're energized by the gap between the current state and what it could become.
Helping individuals, teams, or ideas grow their capability over time — you take the long view and invest in potential before it's obvious.
Noticing the thing that doesn't fit, the opportunity hiding in plain sight, or the detail that changes everything.
Creating clear, accurate accounts of what happened, what was found, or what's at stake — you turn raw information into something useful.
Helping others find their way through unfamiliar or complex situations — you lead from beside, not from above.
Expressing qualities or outcomes in numerical terms to enable comparison and measurement — you believe that if you can measure it, you can manage it.
Creating test conditions or scenarios that mimic real-world situations — you'd rather run the experiment in a safe environment before committing for real.
Constructing explanatory frameworks that make sense of observed patterns — you're not satisfied with knowing what happened until you understand why.
Taking deliberate time to think deeply about experience and extract lasting lessons — you're energized by the act of making meaning from what you've lived.
Improving something through repeated cycles of testing, feedback, and adjustment — you know that the best version is never the first one.
Distributing resources — money, time, people, attention — to where they'll have the greatest impact — you make hard tradeoffs without flinching.
Creating something new and giving it permanence — a norm, a relationship, a standard — you build things meant to last.
Bringing people together in ways that build community, generate shared energy, and create something that couldn't happen alone.
Changing and developing in meaningful ways in response to experience — you're not the same person you were a year ago, and you take pride in that.
Operating at full capability — energized, focused, and producing your best work — you know what conditions bring out your best and you create them.
Reacting to changing conditions or needs quickly, thoughtfully, and effectively — you're at your best when real demands show up unexpectedly.
